steps.title: Compare the new point to the original line
steps.content: At $x=9$ the original line predicts $y=-5.6+8.8(9)=-5.6+79.2=73.6$. The actual new point $(9,52)$ lies $73.6-52=21.6$ pounds below the line.

steps.title: Reason about how the slope must change
steps.content: Adding a point that sits well below the existing line, at an $x$-value far to the right of the original data, pulls the right end of the best-fit line downward. That rotation reduces the slope, so the new slope $s$ must satisfy $s<8.8$.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A) $5.9$ — less than $8.8$, consistent with a flattened line. Matches.
  • B) $8.8$ — equal to the original slope, but adding a point off the line must change it. Eliminated.
  • C) $14.4$ — larger than $8.8$, would require the new point to be above the line. Eliminated.
  • D) $17.8$ — even larger; would require an even steeper line. Eliminated.
That matches choice A — A is correct.
